headline: Civil Service Costs Consume 82% of Operating Budget
context: Treasury data showed that civil service emoluments, pensions, and benefits consumed RM128 billion or 82% of the federal operating budget in 2025, leaving only RM28 billion for supplies, maintenance, and programme delivery.

cards.big: 1.7 million civil servants cost RM128 billion — 82% of operating budget spent before a single programme is delivered
cards.sub: Malaysia has 1 civil servant per 19.4 citizens — compared to Singapore's 1:71 and South Korea's 1:50

cards.big: Pension obligations for retired civil servants at RM32 billion annually will double to RM64 billion by 2040
cards.sub: Defined-benefit pension scheme costs RM1.5 billion more each year — the fastest growing line item in the budget

cards.big: Civil service is 78% Bumiputera — perceived as de facto employment programme rather than efficient governance institution
cards.sub: Average civil servant productivity measured by GDP per government employee is 42% below OECD average

cards.big: Public Service Department proposed reducing 14,000 positions through attrition but cabinet deferred implementation before state elections
cards.sub: Every PM since Mahathir's first tenure promised civil service reform — bloated headcount persists across 8 administrations

cards.big: When 82 sen of every operating ringgit goes to salaries and pensions, the government is running a payroll, not a country
Should Malaysia freeze civil service hiring for 5 years and invest savings in technology to digitise 40% of current manual processes?
